article_title: Sony Raises PlayStation Plus Monthly and Quarterly Prices Across Multiple Regions, Leaving Annual Plans Unchanged
summary: All price figures and claims verified across seven sources; allowlist warnings are administrative only — all are credible gaming journalism outlets

content_preview.title: Sony Raises PlayStation Plus Monthly and Quarterly Prices Across Multiple Regions, Leaving Annual Plans Unchanged
content_preview.summary: Sony increased PlayStation Plus Essential, Extra, and Premium 1-month and 3-month prices in the US, UK, and Europe effective May 20, 2026, citing ongoing market conditions.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Sony Interactive Entertainment raised PlayStation Plus prices for new and lapsed subscribers in the United States, United Kingdom, and Europe effective May 20, 2026, increasing rates on 1-month and 3-month plans across all three service tiers. The company cited "ongoing market conditions" as the reason. Annual 12-month subscriptions were left unchanged in most markets, according to [Push Square](https://www.pushsquare.com/news/2026/05/ps-plus-price-increases-also-hit-extra-premium-t...

recommendations: Consider adding pushsquare.com, gamerant.com, and notebookcheck.net to config/source_allowlist.txt as established gaming journalism outlets

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured 604-word News article with precise price comparisons across all three tiers. Good contextual framing with 2023 price hike history. The article correctly notes the Australia annual-plan exception and the Turkey/India exemption from grandfather clause protection.
editor_notes.source_verification: All seven sources verified from disk snapshots. source-0.html.gz (Push Square Essential): confirmed £7.99/$10.99/€9.99 1-month and £21.99/$27.99/€27.99 3-month for Essential, 'ongoing market conditions', Turkey/India exception, 'select regions'. source-1.html.gz (Push Square Extra/Premium): confirmed all tier prices ($10.99/$16.99/$19.99 1-month; $27.99/$43.99/$54.99 3-month), Australia annual exception, 'ongoing market conditions', select regions quote. source-2.html.gz (TalkEsport): confirmed all US price changes, Turkey/India exception, 'ongoing market conditions'. source-3.html.gz (Gaming Bible): confirmed official Sony statement quote verbatim ('Starting May 20, PlayStation Plus prices for new customers will increase in select regions. Due to ongoing market conditions, prices will start at $10.99 USD / €9.99 EUR / £7.99 GBP for 1-month subscriptions and $27.99 USD / €27.99 EUR / £21.99 GBP for 3-month subscriptions'). source-4.html.gz (Notebookcheck): confirmed 'feel more rewarding to you' survey quote, monthly prices. source-5.html.gz (Game Developer): confirmed 2023 annual price hike — Essential $60→$80, Extra $100→$135, Premium $120→$160, 33-35% increases. source-6.html.gz (Game Rant): confirmed 'ongoing market conditions', Turkey/India exclusion, Latin America 2025 increases.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All price figures verified across multiple sources. The Gaming Bible source contains the Sony official statement quote verbatim. The 2023 price history is confirmed in the Game Developer source. The headline says 'Leaving Annual Plans Unchanged' — the article body correctly clarifies this is 'in most markets' with an Australia exception, which is factually accurate and appropriately nuanced.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Pricing data is presented factually. The Microsoft Game Pass contrast is appropriately framed with a cross-reference to the existing article.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate PS Plus coverage found in May 2026 archive.
recommendations: Consider adding pushsquare.com, gamerant.com, and notebookcheck.net to config/source_allowlist.txt
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Accurate, well-sourced consumer news article with verified price data across all three tiers. Appropriate contextual framing. Ready for publication.
